title: Bidens setigera
taxonomy: Bidens setigera is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Astereae, and its genus, Bidens, is a group of annual or perennial plants. The species Bidens setigera is native to North America, specifically in the eastern and central regions. The classification of Bidens setigera is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The species is also known by the common name tickseed coreopsis, which is a nod to its unique characteristics.
uses: Bidens setigera has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are thought to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In some cultures, Bidens setigera is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. The plant's extracts may also have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential applications of Bidens setigera.

intro: Bidens setigera, also known as Blackjack, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens setigera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bidens setigera is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, with the most common preparations being a tea to soothe respiratory issues and a tincture to aid in wound healing.
